question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, showing that CPI increased only 0.3% in March 2026 on a seasonally adjusted basis. This is definitively less than the 0.8% threshold required for YES resolution. The market question asks if CPI will rise 'more than 0.8%' and the actual result was 0.3%, making this a clear NO resolution.
key_factors: Multiple credible sources confirm the 0.3% figure
risks: Misinterpretation of seasonal vs non-seasonal adjustment
